metadata.notes: agent=yes daemon=full driver=gap:sigkill тАФ the foreground orphan IS producible (SIGKILL parent mid-child). Commit 3d89d5e9 added deriveVibeParentSessionID, so the child is now LINKED off its <parent>/agents/<child>/ path; with the link the existing orphan machinery holds the parent working while the child runs and reaps both on the shared-PID SIGKILL. Route driver-gap: sigkill is stubbed to not_implemented, record ports the seam.

assessment.body:
## Verdict (RE-ASSESSMENT тАФ daemon bugтЖТfull)

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full (was `bug`: the child was observed but UNLINKED. Commit 3d89d5e9 added `deriveVibeParentSessionID` to the parent-derivation chain, deriving the parent straight off the `.../<parent>/agents/<child>/messages.jsonl` path; the child now surfaces LINKED, and the existing orphan machinery тАФ parent held working while the child runs, both reaped on the shared-PID SIGKILL тАФ applies unchanged)
driver_capability: gap:sigkill (the interactive driver stubs `sigkill` to `not_implemented`; `DRIVE_ELICITS='send slash sleep'` omits it)
тЖТ route: **driver-gap** тАФ `record` ports the `sigkill` step from the claudecode driver, then records a CLEAN pass (no more `known_failing`). Same driver-gap route as before, but the daemon pillar is now `full`.

## What changed since the prior assessment (bug тЖТ full)

The prior verdict was `daemon=bug`: the vibe child was minted as its own session but surfaced UNLINKED тАФ `parser.go` set no `ParentSessionID`, `sidecar.go` read the child `meta.json` yet ignored its `parent_session_id`, and `deriveParentSession` had no vibe rule. So `parent_linked` never fired and the scenario's "тЙе1 child linked to a parent" observable failed.

Commit 3d89d5e9 (`feat(vibe): link Task-tool subagents to their parent session`) closes exactly that gap. `deriveVibeParentSessionID` (core/application/services/helpers.go:148) is wired into `deriveParentSession` (helpers.go:124), and `session_detector_activity.go:156` calls `deriveParentSession(ev.TranscriptPath)` for any new session with an empty `ev.ParentSessionID`, stamping `state.ParentSessionID` on the child. The derivation reads the parent straight off the path:

- child transcript: `~/.vibe/logs/session/<parent>/agents/<child>/messages.jsonl`
- `deriveVibeParentSessionID` walks up: `childDir=.../<child>`, `agentsDir=.../agents` (guarded `Base=="agents"`), returns `<parent>` = `Base(Dir(agentsDir))`.
- The vibe adapter registers a session under its DIRECTORY name (`sessionIDFromPath` = `Base(Dir(messages.jsonl))`), so the parent's registered `SessionID` IS `<parent>` тАФ the derived id matches the registered parent exactly.
- The child's own `meta.json parent_session_id` is deliberately NOT used (it holds the parent's internal UUID, not its directory name тАФ it would never match the registered parent). Path-derivation is the correct source.
- Top-level guard: a top-level vibe session's grandparent dir is `session`, not `agents`, so `deriveVibeParentSessionID` returns "" тАФ no false parent. Unit-covered (`helpers_internal_test.go`, incl. the no-false-parent case); `go test ./core/application/services -run 'Vibe|ParentSession'` passes.

## agent_supports = yes (unchanged тАФ synchronous Task model does NOT block the FOREGROUND orphan)

The orphan is produced by SIGKILLing the parent WHILE the synchronous child is still in flight, not by a fire-and-forget dispatch (that reasoning is why 3-2_background-subagent is agent=no, but not 3.4). `core/tools/builtins/task.py` `Task.run()` builds a child `AgentLoop(is_subagent=True)`, sets `parent_session_id=ctx.session_id`, and awaits `subagent_loop.act(task_text)`; while the parent's Task tool-call blocks on that child loop, the child is writing its own `messages.jsonl` under the watched root. A SIGKILL of the vibe process group during that window strands the child mid-run тАФ the orphan precondition. The child's own "wait 30s" keeps it in flight so the kill lands before it drains. vibe 2.19.0 ships the Task tool by default; `task(agent=explore)` auto-executes (allowlist ALWAYS, no permission prompt). vibe runs the subagent IN-PROCESS, so parent+child share ONE PID тАФ not a disqualifier (claudecode's Task subagent is in-process too and is agent=yes/daemon=full); the "orphan" is a child SESSION record on a dead PID, reaped generically.

## daemon_capability = full (child observed, LINKED, and reaped without a spurious working transition)

Four facts, the first three carried over and the fourth now closed:

1. **Child is observed (`transcript_new` fires).** The child lands at `~/.vibe/logs/session/<parent>/agents/<explore_...>/messages.jsonl` тАФ inside `Source = FilesUnderRoot{~/.vibe/logs/session}`. `sessionIDFromPath` accepts an arbitrarily-nested `messages.jsonl` and mints a distinct session keyed on the child dir (`Base(Dir(path))` = `<child>`). So `child_session_birth` PASSES.
2. **The parent link now BINDS (the fix).** `deriveVibeParentSessionID` derives `<parent>` off the path; `<parent>` is the parent's registered `SessionID`; `session_detector_activity.go` stamps it as `state.ParentSessionID` and records the parent-child linkage. `parent_linked` now FIRES; `SubagentCount(parent) тЙе 1`. This is the observable that was failing and is now satisfied тЖТ drop `known_failing`.
3. **The orphan reap works.** PID discovery is by cwd+cmdline (`pid.go DiscoverPIDByCWDAndCmdLine`): the child's cwd = the parent's cwd, so BOTH sessions resolve to the SAME vibe PID. When that PID is SIGKILLed, the `pid_manager` liveness sweep finds the PID gone (ESRCH) and emits `process_exited` for both session rows; the orphaned child is a session on a dead PID, swept without any spurious `working` transition (the #321 invariant is adapter-agnostic). `parent_killed` and `child_cleanup` PASS.
4. **The link makes the existing orphan machinery apply.** Now that `ParentSessionID` is set, `session_detector_subagent.go finishOrphanedChildren` holds the parent working while the child runs and fast-forwards the child on the parent's turn-done, and `pid_manager` (issue #757: children carry "parent deleted") reaps children when the parent is reaped. The whole subagent lifecycle is now generic-machinery territory тАФ no vibe-specific gap remains on the daemon side.

## driver_capability = gap:sigkill (unchanged)

The recipe (send the Task prompt, sleep for the child to materialize, SIGKILL the process group, sleep for the sweep) needs send/sleep/**sigkill**. `replaydata/agents/mistral-vibe/driver-interactive.sh` declares `DRIVE_ELICITS='send slash sleep'` and routes `sigkill)` to `not_implemented` (TODO: kill -9 the active slot's PID). send + sleep are elicited; `sigkill` is the one missing step тЖТ `driver=gap:sigkill`. `record` ports the `sigkill` seam from the claudecode reference driver before it drives.

## What record settles

The live spawn+SIGKILL confirms: the recursive tailer mints the nested child, `deriveVibeParentSessionID` binds it to the parent on the live on-disk dir shape, both sessions resolve to the one vibe PID via cwd+cmdline discovery, and both reap on the shared PID's death with no spurious working transition. Because the fix is doc/code-confirmed and unit-tested but NOT yet live-recorded for this exact nested shape, confidence is held at 0.8.
caveats: daemon=full is code/unit-confirmed but not yet LIVE-recorded for the nested vibe subagent shape: no live ~/.vibe/logs/session session with an agents/ subtree exists on disk (none of the captured sessions used the Task tool). The child layout and the shared-PID reap are inferred from task.py + session_logger.py + the adapter source and validated by the fix's unit tests (helpers_internal_test.go) тАФ confidence held at 0.8. A single live spawn+SIGKILL settles the on-disk shape, confirms deriveVibeParentSessionID binds on the real path, and confirms both sessions reap on the one vibe PID without a spurious working transition. This is exactly what record promotes from provisional to settled.

The link derivation binds only when ev.ParentSessionID is empty (session_detector_activity.go: parentID = deriveParentSession only if ev.ParentSessionID == ""). The vibe parser sets no ParentSessionID, so the path-derivation always runs for vibe children тАФ but if a future adapter change started emitting a (wrong) ParentSessionID it would win over the path derivation. Not a current risk; noted for the record backflow.

cost is not asserted: mistral-vibe is absent from core/pkg/capacity and sidecar.go never reads stats.session_cost, so the daemon emits no cost. observations assert model + tokens + agent only, cost_nonzero=false тАФ matching the vibe 2-1/2-3/3-1 siblings.

model mistral-medium-3.5 is from the live config.active_model observed on prior vibe recordings; if the account default has changed since, record corrects it from the recording. tokens_nonzero comes from sidecar stats.context_tokens surfacing on turn_done.

Contrast with 3-2_background-subagent (agent=no): there the synchronous model IS the blocker (no fire-and-forget тЖТ the parent can never end_turn before the child drains). Here the synchronous model is NOT a blocker because the orphan is produced by a hard SIGKILL mid-child. Same distinction as gemini-cli 3-4 тАФ except gemini's child is not observed at all (daemon=bug there), whereas vibe's child is observed AND now linked (daemon=full).

RECORDING FINDING (2026-07-07-22-42-13_irrlichd-0.5.5+3bc1709.dirty, 3/8, known_failing): the sigkill-orphan sequence is NOT reliably reproducible for mistral-vibe. vibe's foreground subagent is SYNCHRONOUS + in-process and completes near-instantly (child agent finished turn -> ready within ~7ms of birth; even a generated essay finished in ~4s), so across both attempts the child was reaped by the normal child-ready liveness sweep BEFORE the SIGKILL landed тАФ it was never a lingering orphan at parent-kill. Both sessions ARE still cleaned up (child transcript_removed via liveness sweep, parent process_exited+transcript_removed on the PID exit) тАФ no leak; parent_linked fires correctly. Secondary: vibe's atomic per-turn flush surfaced the child transcript_new BEFORE the parent's, so the offline validator's new_session match for parent_session_birth grabbed the child session (which never goes working), cascading the arc. daemon cleanup is correct (full); the scenario as specified (child reaped AS a consequence of the parent SIGKILL) cannot be staged for a synchronous fast subagent. known_failing pending a vibe detached/long-running subagent variant or an event-timed (not sleep-timed) kill.

sources.note: THE FIX: deriveVibeParentSessionID (l.148) derives the parent off the .../<parent>/agents/<child>/messages.jsonl path and is wired into deriveParentSession (l.124); returns the parent DIR name = the parent's registered SessionID (sessionIDFromPath)

l.156: for a new session with empty ev.ParentSessionID, parentID = deriveParentSession(ev.TranscriptPath), stamped onto state.ParentSessionID and recorded as parent-child linkage тЖТ parent_linked now fires for vibe children
unit tests for deriveVibeParentSessionID incl. the top-level no-false-parent case; go test ./core/application/services -run 'Vibe|ParentSession' passes

Source = FilesUnderRoot{~/.vibe/logs/session}; sessionIDFromPath = Base(Dir(messages.jsonl)) mints the child under its own <child> dir at any depth (recursive tailer observes the nested child); the parent registers under <parent> тАФ the id deriveVibeParentSessionID returns
DiscoverPID by cwd+cmdline (DiscoverPIDByCWDAndCmdLine); child shares the parent's cwd тЖТ both sessions resolve to the one vibe PID, so the SIGKILL sweep reaps both
finishOrphanedChildren holds the parent working while children run and fast-forwards a child on the parent's turn-done тАФ now reachable for vibe because ParentSessionID is set

liveness sweep reaps a bound PID that returns ESRCH and emits process_exited; children of a reaped parent carry 'parent deleted' (issue #757) тАФ the #321 no-spurious-working invariant is adapter-agnostic

Task.run() builds child AgentLoop(is_subagent=True), sets parent_session_id=ctx.session_id, awaits subagent_loop.act(); SessionLoggingConfig(save_dir=ctx.session_dir/'agents', session_prefix=args.agent) тАФ synchronous in-flight child under the watched root

sibling precedent: synchronous/awaited subagent is agent=yes for the FOREGROUND orphan variant (SIGKILL mid-child); gemini's child is NOT observed (daemon=bug) whereas vibe's is now observed AND linked (daemon=full)
DRIVE_ELICITS='send slash sleep'; sigkill) routed to not_implemented тЖТ driver=gap:sigkill

preconditions: vibe CLI 2.19.0 on PATH and authenticated; the Task tool is exposed by default and agent=explore auto-executes (allowlist ALWAYS)
irrlichd is recording (dev daemon with --record, isolated IRRLICHT_HOME) at a build that includes commit 3d89d5e9 (deriveVibeParentSessionID) тАФ required for parent_linked to fire

DRIVER GAP: driver-interactive.sh routes sigkill to not_implemented (DRIVE_ELICITS='send slash sleep'); record ports the sigkill seam (kill -9 the active slot's process group, not just the parent PID) before driving

the sigkill step must kill the ENTIRE vibe process group so the in-flight child is orphaned rather than reaped by its own clean exit; vibe runs the subagent in-process so parent+child share one PID/process group
setup: Driver generates a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/

A README.md with a single H1 heading is seeded into the cwd so the explore subagent has a deterministic file to read while the parent is alive тАФ the subagent must still be in flight when SIGKILL lands

RESHAPE (record): empty staging cwd, so the seeded-README child task cannot run тАФ replaced with a self-contained long-essay explore-subagent task (>=1500 words, ~30-40s) so the child is reliably alive and observed when the parent is SIGKILLed. Pre-sigkill sleep raised 12s->22s to clear vibe boot variance (dir has appeared anywhere from +4s to +21s) so the child is minted+linked before the kill. bypass_tool_permissions=true so the Task tool auto-executes.

script.text: Use your Task tool to launch a subagent (agent: explore) whose ONLY job is to run this exact bash command and wait for it to finish: sleep 45 тАФ then report the word ready. Do not reply to me until the subagent is completely finished.

verify: the parent session enters ready, then readyтЖТworking on the user Task prompt
a child session materializes under <parent>/agents/<explore_...>/messages.jsonl and is minted as a session (transcript_new)
events.jsonl contains тЙе1 parent_linked binding the child to the parent session_id (deriveVibeParentSessionID off the path)
events.jsonl contains exactly one process_exited for the parent session and one for the (orphaned) child session after the SIGKILL
no state_transition to working (or waiting) fires for the child after the parent's process_exited (issue #321 regression invariant)
final parent state is ready; after readyTTL both rows are removed via transcript_removed
